title: A Mother's Fight for Justice After a Funeral Mistake
summary: Jasmine Beverley is a mother who lost her baby boy, Sunny, before he was born. A funeral director named Robert Bush handled Sunny's funeral but made a big mistake. He gave families the wrong ashes and kept bodies at his place for too long. Jasmine got Sunny's ashes in the same box she had used for him, which made her suspicious. Later, police found Sunny's remains at Bush's funeral home. Jasmine was very sad and worried during her next pregnancy. Bush admitted to doing wrong things in court. Now, Jasmine wants to change the law so this doesn't happen to other families. She believes Bush will face consequences for his actions.

background_read: This story is about a mother named Jasmine Beverley whose baby, Sunny, died before birth. A funeral director, Robert Bush, made mistakes by giving wrong ashes to families and not burying bodies properly. Jasmine noticed something was wrong when she got Sunny's ashes in the same box she had used. Later, police found Sunny's body at Bush's funeral home. Jasmine felt very upset, especially when she was pregnant again. Bush admitted his mistakes in court, and Jasmine is now working to change laws to protect other families. It shows how important it is to be honest and careful when handling funerals.
Article_Structure: WHO: Jasmine Beverley, a mother who lost her baby Sunny, and Robert Bush, a funeral director who made mistakes.
WHAT: Bush gave families the wrong ashes and kept bodies too long, while Jasmine is campaigning to change the law.
WHERE: This happened in Hull, at a funeral home called Legacy Independent Funeral Directors.
WHY: Jasmine wants justice and to prevent similar mistakes, as Bush's actions caused her and others pain.

perspectives.description: She feels hurt and wants the funeral director to be held responsible for his actions.
